tasks.title: Document decK diff before sync
tasks.content:
A production gateway workflow should run `deck gateway diff` before `deck gateway sync`. The diff gives operators a chance to review route, service, plugin, and upstream changes before applying them.

Recommended flow:

- render desired Kong state
- run decK diff against the target gateway
- review changes in CI logs or PR output
- apply sync only after the target backend is healthy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows gateway-as-code discipline and avoids blind configuration pushes.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm diff happens before sync.

tasks.title: Add rate limiting plugin notes
tasks.content:
Rate limiting belongs at the gateway boundary for public or shared internal APIs. Kong can enforce request limits before traffic reaches backend services.

Plugin planning inputs:

- consumer or service-level scope
- limit period and burst tolerance
- storage policy
- response behavior when limits are exceeded
- dashboard and alerting signals
tasks.portfolio_reason: Connects Kong gateway configuration with production traffic protection.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it describes planning inputs instead of hard-coded policy.

tasks.title: Add request size limit notes
tasks.content:
Request size limits reduce backend risk by rejecting oversized payloads at the gateway. This is useful for APIs that accept uploads, JSON bodies, or AI prompt payloads.

Operational considerations:

- define limits per route or service
- align limits with upstream application constraints
- log rejected requests for troubleshooting
- document expected client behavior

files.content:

- log rejected requests for troubleshooting
- document expected client behavior

tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows practical API gateway hardening beyond simple routing.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it describes route/service scope.

tasks.title: Document Kong Admin API security
tasks.content:
The Kong Admin API should never be exposed as a public endpoint. Automation should reach it through trusted networks or CI runners with controlled access.

Baseline controls:

- bind Admin API to localhost or private network
- restrict access by firewall or network policy
- avoid sharing admin credentials in repository files
- apply decK changes from trusted automation only
tasks.portfolio_reason: Highlights a key production security boundary for Kong operations.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it does not suggest public Admin API exposure.

files.content:

- restrict access by firewall or network policy
- avoid sharing admin credentials in repository files
- apply decK changes from trusted automation only

tasks.title: Add route promotion checklist
tasks.content:
Route promotion should happen only after the target backend is running and healthy.

Checklist:

- start inactive backend color
- wait for container health
- render decK state to target color
- run decK diff
- sync gateway state
- verify traffic through Kong proxy
- stop old color only after verification
tasks.portfolio_reason: Makes the zero-downtime gateway story reviewable and repeatable.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it preserves the old backend until after verification.

- verify traffic through Kong proxy
- stop old color only after verification

tasks.title: Add gateway observability notes
tasks.content:
Gateway observability should show whether failures are caused by routing, upstream services, client behavior, or gateway policy.

Useful signals:

- request count by route and status code
- upstream latency
- gateway latency
- rate-limit rejections
- request size rejections
- Admin API change history through CI logs
tasks.portfolio_reason: Connects Kong operations with monitoring and incident diagnosis.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm the signals support troubleshooting.

files.content:
Gateway observability should show whether failures are caused by routing, upstream services, client behavior, or gateway policy.

Useful signals:

- request count by route and status code
- upstream latency
- gateway latency
- rate-limit rejections
- request size rejections
- Admin API change history through CI logs

tasks.title: Add gateway plugin scope policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows production API gateway controls are attached deliberately to route, service, or consumer scope.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm unscoped plugins are rejected.

files.content:
Gateway plugins should have an intentional scope. Unscoped plugins can change global gateway behavior and should require explicit review.

Accepted scopes:

- route
- service
- consumer
- global only with explicit approval

tasks.title: Add upstream health policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Connects Kong route promotion with active upstream health checks.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm active and passive health checks are required.

files.content:
Kong upstreams should expose health policy before traffic promotion. Active checks catch broken backends before traffic moves, while passive checks help detect runtime failures.

Required policy:

- active health check path
- healthy threshold
- unhealthy threshold
- passive failure detection

tasks.title: Add decK change summary helper
tasks.portfolio_reason: Makes gateway-as-code changes easier to review before sync.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm route, service, and plugin counts are summarized.

files.content:
decK diff output should be summarized for reviewers so route, service, and plugin changes are visible before sync. A small summary helper can make CI output easier to scan.

Summary fields:

- routes changed
- services changed
- plugins changed
- upstreams changed

tasks.title: Add route host policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Keeps gateway route exposure intentional by requiring host and path constraints.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm wildcard routes are rejected unless explicitly allowed.

files.content:
Gateway routes should define host and path constraints so services are not accidentally exposed through broad catch-all routes.

Policy checks:

- route has a host
- route has a path
- wildcard host requires explicit approval
- strip path behavior is documented

tasks.title: Add consumer quota policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows gateway governance for shared APIs and tenant-aware traffic protection.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm quota limits must be positive.

files.content:
Consumer quota policy should define per-consumer request budgets before plugins are applied. This keeps shared API limits predictable and auditable.

Policy fields:

- consumer name
- requests per minute
- burst allowance
- alert threshold

tasks.title: Add JWT plugin policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows gateway authentication plugins are configured with explicit claims and algorithms.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm weak JWT policies are rejected.

files.content:
JWT plugin policy should define expected claims and allowed algorithms before a route is exposed. This keeps auth behavior reviewable in gateway-as-code.

Policy checks:

- required claims
- allowed algorithms
- anonymous access setting
- clock skew tolerance

tasks.title: Add gateway timeout policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows route protection includes bounded upstream timeouts.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm missing or excessive timeouts are reported.

files.content:
Gateway timeout policy protects callers and upstreams from unbounded waits. Route and service definitions should keep connect, read, and write timeouts explicit.

Policy fields:

- connect timeout
- read timeout
- write timeout
- maximum allowed timeout
